subtitle: Arm swing tension and rotator cuff fatigue

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Shoulder fatigue accumulates on runs over 2 hours

mild.description: Aching or tension in the shoulder during or after long runs
mild.can_run_note: Yes — consciously relax arm swing every few km
why: Arms crossing the midline during running creating rotational strain
Shoulder shrugging under fatigue at miles 12 and beyond
Rotator cuff fatigue from sustained arm swing effort
relief: Shoulder rolls and cross-body stretch immediately post-run
Massage trapezius and posterior shoulder with a ball against a wall
Heat pad on shoulder for 15 min
Rest from overhead lifting 24–48 hours after long runs
prevention: Arms should swing front-to-back, not across the body
Keep hands loose — imagine holding a crisp between your fingers
Elbows at approximately 90 degrees, not clenched
Shoulder mobility routine — 5 min before long runs

moderate.description: Persistent ache, limited range of motion, painful overhead
moderate.can_run_note: Run but avoid any overhead gym work until resolved
why: Rotator cuff tendinopathy from repetitive low-load stress
Subacromial impingement from poor shoulder mechanics under fatigue
Pectoral tightness pulling the shoulder into internal rotation
relief: Pendulum swings — lean forward, let arm hang and circle gently
prevention: External rotation band work 3× per week
Strengthen lower trapezius and serratus anterior
Check running posture — avoid rounded upper back
Strengthen thoracic extension with foam roller

severe.description: Sharp pain with any arm movement, unable to lift arm
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — arm swing will aggravate the injury
why: Possible rotator cuff tear or significant tendon damage
Labral involvement needing imaging to assess
relief: Complete rest from all upper body loading
Imaging if no improvement after 2 weeks
Corticosteroid injection may be considered for bursitis
prevention: Full rotator cuff rehabilitation before returning to running
Gradual return to arm swing — start with arm in sling if needed
Address thoracic kyphosis as a contributing factor

see_physio_if: Unable to lift arm above shoulder height
Sharp catching pain with specific movements
